name: Narsil MCP Security Platform
what_it_does: Narsil MCP server providing AI agents with security assessment and threat analysis capabilities — enabling LLM-powered security workflows to analyze code, configurations, and systems for vulnerabilities, perform security assessments, and integrate security intelligence into agent-driven security operations pipelines.
use_cases: Analyzing code for security vulnerabilities from security audit agents
Performing configuration security assessments from compliance agents
Integrating security intelligence into AI-driven SOC workflows
Automating vulnerability triage and analysis from security agents
Building security-aware coding assistants that check for common flaws
Threat modeling and risk assessment from security planning agents
not_for: Teams without security expertise to interpret results
Automated attack tooling (assessment, not offensive use)
best_when: A security team needs AI-assisted code or configuration security assessment integrated into their agent workflow.
avoid_when: You need certified compliance scanning tools or enterprise SAST — use commercial alternatives like Snyk, Semgrep, or Checkmarx.

auth.notes: No external authentication. Runs as local analysis tool. May integrate with external security APIs depending on configuration.

pricing.notes: Free, open source community MCP for security assessment. Self-hosted.

known_agent_gotchas: Community security tool — validate findings before acting on them
Security assessment results need expert interpretation — not plug-and-play
May produce false positives — requires human review for production decisions
Analyze only code/systems you have permission to assess
Community MCP with limited track record — audit before production use
Security tool quality varies widely — test against known vulnerabilities first
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local security assessment tool. No external API calls by default. Community MCP. Validate all findings. Only use on authorized targets. Sensitive findings should not be logged to external services.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local tool. No credentials needed. Community MCP. Analyze authorized targets only.
